summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on May 28, 2024, with two trustees present. Key actions included approving out-of-town travel for school resource officers, promoting Division Chief Kevin Lynch to deputy chief of the fire department, selecting Conger Construction as the construction manager for the demolition and reconstruction of Station 18, appointing a new full-time police officer, and setting the deputy fire chief salary. The board also recognized the success of the Memorial Day parade and service and announced an upcoming community cleanup day.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for May 15–28, 2024
Approved out-of-town travel for SRO Groves and SRO Alcorn to attend NASRO training in Phoenix, AZ, July 14–19, at no cost (grant funded)
Promoted Division Chief Kevin Lynch to Deputy Chief effective June 3, 2024
Selected Conger Construction as construction manager for demolition and reconstruction of Station 18 and approved the associated contract
Adjourned into executive session at 6:09 p.m. per ORC 121.22 G1
Appointed Robert Lyons as full-time police officer effective June 17, 2024, with salary commensurate with experience
Set deputy fire chief salary at $93,691.20
Adjourned the regular meeting at 6:24 p.m.
action_items: Alyssa to finalize new property maintenance code contract based on Julia's markup
Chad and service department to prepare for community cleanup day on Saturday
Chief Myers to proceed with Conger Construction on Station 18 demolition and reconstruction
Chief Rose to onboard Robert Lyons as full-time police officer effective June 17, 2024
notable_topics: Memorial Day parade and service recognition
Community cleanup day scheduled for Saturday 8 a.m. to noon
New medic unit delivery expected late June/early July (replacement for unit totaled last July)
SRO training travel approval (grant funded, no cost to township)
Promotion of Kevin Lynch to Deputy Fire Chief
Selection of Conger Construction for Station 18 demolition and reconstruction
Hiring of new police officer Robert Lyons to fill one of two vacancies
Property maintenance code update in progress
Joe Reynolds returned to full staff after gallbladder surgery
attendees: Kevin Lynch (promoted to Deputy Fire Chief)
notes: Trustee Hodges was absent (roll called but no response recorded). The meeting included a moment of prayer for the Flint family and Clinton Massey community regarding the loss of Jason Flint. The executive session lasted from 6:09 p.m. to 6:23 p.m. and resulted in the police officer appointment and deputy fire chief salary decisions. The cleanup day location has moved to the current facility rather than the green property across the street.
title: BOT 5-28-24
